gates.name: leakage_game_pk
gates.measured: 0 shared game_pks across all splits (train=1000, val=300, test=300 game_pks)

gates.name: leakage_pitcher_train_test
gates.threshold: no pitcher in both train and test (per spec Ticket 1)
gates.measured: 409 shared pitcher_ids between train and test cohorts (475 train/val); season-disjoint splits do not exclude returning pitchers

gates.name: ablation_overall
gates.measured: 0/3 variants pass; identity_only test_ppl=52.99 < full test_ppl=148.47

ablation_summary.verdict: FAIL — identity_only collapsed to test_ppl=52.99 (3x BETTER than full at 148.47), indicating pitcher-identity embedding alone dominates the signal at the 1K-game/5-epoch scale. tokens_only and count_only land within ±0.3% of full — context features are not pulling weight.

calibration_summary.verdict: PASS — both pre- and post-temperature ECE comfortably below the 0.10 spec gate. Temperature scaling halves ECE from 0.0138 to 0.0068.

notes: Game_pk leakage gate PASSes via disjoint season splits (2015-2022 / 2023 / 2024). Pitcher-identity leakage gate FAILS by spec letter — 409 pitchers appear in both train and test cohorts, but this is structural (the same pitchers play across years) and is a known limitation of season-based splitting; the spec asks for a stronger pitcher-disjoint split that the current dataset loader does not implement. Ablation gate FAILS hard: identity_only is BETTER than full (52.99 < 148.47, 180% gap in the wrong direction), and tokens_only/count_only differ from full by less than 1% — at 1K train games / 5 epochs, the pitcher embedding overfits to a near-perfect train-set fingerprint, while the contextual features add no measurable lift at this scale. Calibration is excellent (ECE 0.014 pre-T, 0.007 post-T). Last night's 10K-game ablation reported a 4.14% gap (still <10%); today's 1K-game run shows the gap inverted — both runs FAIL Ticket 6, with the 1K result amplifying the identity-dominance pathology. CausalWAR validation running concurrently in another agent — untouched by this run.
